_core_paths_doc: BLUE crates per CLAUDE.md. ade_runtime is RED (shell); ade_testkit and ade_node are neither. ade_network has per-submodule BLUE scoping: mux::frame is BLUE; mux::transport and session are RED. ade_core::consensus (S-B1 onward) is BLUE under the already-listed crate prefix. Substring matches against absolute file paths.
_replay_cmd_doc: ade_testkit hosts boundary_replay, differential_*_replay, ledger_determinism, and stateful_replay test suites.
_replay_on_stop_doc: Disabled by default. Phase 4 test runtime is acceptable but full replay across all eras is not sub-second; flip to true once selective replay is introduced.
_clusters_dir_doc: Canonical IDD location. Currently contains PHASE4-N-D (in-flight). Closed Phase 0A/0B/1/2A/2C clusters remain at docs/completed/phase_*/ as historical anchors.
_cluster_id_format_doc: Cluster IDs use the PHASE<N>-<LETTER> convention (e.g., PHASE4-N-A, PHASE4-N-D). Slash commands pass the cluster id as the literal directory name under docs/clusters/.
_clusters_archive_dir_doc: Where /cluster-close moves cluster docs upon closure. Created on demand.
_planning_doc_doc: Active phase plan. /cluster-doc reads this when seeding new cluster docs (N-A, N-B, N-C, N-E, N-F).
_grounding_docs_dir_doc: Where the four grounding docs live: ade-CODEMAP.md, ade-SEAMS.md, ade-HEAD_DELTAS.md, ade-TRACEABILITY.md.

AE.F impl: pump_block (the RED durable-admit chokepoint in ade_runtime::forward_sync::pump), immediately after decode_block and BEFORE the BLUE chokepoint reducer, queries db.get_block_by_hash(&decoded.block_hash); if Some(stored) AND stored.slot == decoded.header_input.slot it returns Ok(None) (idempotent no-op -- no reducer step, no WAL append, no tip change; replay-equivalent). HASH-keyed (never slot-alone): a DIFFERENT block (different hash) at/before the last-applied slot returns None, falls through to the UNCHANGED BLUE authority, and fails closed (SlotBeforeLastApplied / BlockNoOutOfOrder). HEADLINE: the RED producer signing shell now EVOLVES the operator KES key forward to the requested period before signing (reusing the existing deterministic Sum6KES update), so the forge works ACROSS KES periods instead of only at the minted period 0 -- the minted-at-period-0 key would otherwise fail KesPeriodNotCurrent once the chain's KES period > 0. The item-4 C1 re-run proved it live: with the fix Ade forged 3 period-1 blocks + self-accepted them, and the real cardano-node DOWNLOADED the period-1 header with NO KES/parse rejection (pre-fix: failed=5/succeeded=0, KesPeriodNotCurrent). Both gating reviews PASS (per-slice + per-cluster; NO HIGH+); two pre-existing fail-closed INFO items handed to C2 (kes_advance_to zeroes the key on a FAILED advance -- unreachable via the forge, fail-safe; opcert_start+63 can diverge from the absolute Sum6KES ceiling 63 when opcert_start>0 -- still fail-closed, C2 must derive kes_max_period from the absolute ceiling). GENESIS-WINDOW FINDING (recorded honestly): slotsPerKESPeriod 129600 == genesis density window 3k/f 129600, so KES period 1 begins exactly when the genesis window closes -- a from-genesis rehearsal can never show forge-at-period-1 AND follower-adopt simultaneously (the period-1 follower rejection is CandidateTooSparse, a genesis-density-window limit, KES-INDEPENDENT, not a KES rejection); cross-period end-to-end forge->adopt is the C2 tip path (dense current tip, no genesis window), NOT a net reset (which returns to period 0). NARROW claim: peer-driven memory bounded BEFORE authoritative decode/apply -- NOT full network DoS resistance, NOT peer fairness, NOT BA-02/live-evidence readiness. This baseline (`6f848825`) is the N-F-G-E slice-span HEAD; the next cluster's /head-deltas measures from here. --- HISTORICAL (prior N-F-G-C baseline 90791691, PHASE4-N-F-G-C S2, 2026-06-02). HEAD_DELTAS for the N-F-G-C close narrated the `339cccb1 -> 90791691` span: the G-B close tail `febee120` + the G-C cluster (S1 live NodeBlockSource::WirePump feed on the --mode node On arm via the reused closed dial_for_admission+run_admission_wire_pump; S2 NEW RED ade_node::ba02_pass BA-02 evidence wiring over the GREEN correlate). 117->118 CI (+ci_check_ba02_evidence_manifest_schema.sh; ci_check_served_chain_handoff_fence.sh broadened in place); 313 rules unchanged; 456 canonical types unchanged (NO BLUE change). RO-LIVE-01 stays partial / RO-LIVE-06 stays enforced -- G-C closes the MECHANICAL live-feed + evidence scaffolding ONLY; the operator-witnessed live ACCEPT is still owed (blocked_until_operator_stake_available). This baseline (`90791691`) is the N-F-G-C slice-span HEAD; the next cluster's /head-deltas measures from here. --- HISTORICAL (prior N-F-G-B baseline 339cccb1, PHASE4-N-F-G-B S3 gate fix, 2026-06-01). HEAD_DELTAS for the N-F-G-B close narrated the `80dac1f7 -> 339cccb1` span (the G-A close tail -- `62cb8718` G-A close + `1806584c` gate-binding follow-up -- plus the PHASE4-N-F-G-B self-accept->serve handoff cluster on the `--mode node` spine: S1 GREEN `SelfAcceptedHandoff` carrier (newtype over the BLUE `AcceptedBlock`, private field, sole ctor `from_self_accepted`) + `run_real_forge` wrapper-split surfacing the original self-accepted token via a sibling return component (NO new `CoordinatorEvent` variant); S2 dispatcher-spawned sibling served-chain admit task draining a typed `mpsc<SelfAcceptedHandoff>` to the single `ServedChainHandle::push_atomic` fed only by `into_accepted()` (relay-loop body forwards a typed channel send only; containment gate byte-unchanged); S3 hermetic loopback proving the served block-fetch payload tag-24-unwraps to the self-accept input bytes + new additive gate `ci_check_served_chain_handoff_fence.sh`; 10 commits / +3391 / -1600). DC-NODE-06 flipped declared->enforced at close (+ 2 strengthenings: CN-PROD-04, CN-CONS-07); 1 new CI gate (116->117). NO BLUE crate changed (456 canonical types unchanged); 313 registry rules (count unchanged); 1 new GREEN module (`ade_runtime::producer::self_accepted_handoff`). No real socket/peer wired -- hermetic loopback only; live serve + operator-peer ACCEPT = G-C (RO-LIVE-01/06 stay partial). This baseline (`339cccb1`) is the N-F-G-B slice-span HEAD; the next cluster's /head-deltas measures its span from here. Bump on each cluster close to keep HEAD_DELTAS per-cluster and reviewable.
_normative_docs_doc: Files /traceability reads to extract rules. Tier-gate statement and its Tier 5 addendum carry the most enforced rules; the contract docs anchor cross-surface invariants; CLAUDE.md carries the commit-attribution policy.
_canonical_type_registry_doc: No separate canonical-type registry file yet; canonical-type rules live inline in the invariant registry under family T.
_invariant_registry_doc: Canonical, append-only invariant registry. Schema follows ziranity-v3's constitution_rule_registry.toml (base fields identical), additively compatible with the IDD template. 341 entries at HEAD (209 enforced / 20 partial / 112 declared / 0 deprecated) across families T (true), CN (constraint network), DC (derived constraint), OP (operational), RO (release obligation). Recent additions through PHASE4-N-AE.F (enforced): DC-NODE-16 (receive idempotency at the durable-admit chokepoint -- a peer-delivered block already durably present byte-identically in the ChainDb, same slot AND same hash, is an idempotent no-op at pump_block: no validation step, no WAL append, no tip change, replay-equivalent; a DIFFERENT block, different hash, at/before the last-applied slot is NOT short-circuited and reaches the unchanged BLUE header authority and fails closed; gated on HASH equality vs the durable store, never slot alone; closes the post-CE-A5 echo where the relay re-announced Ade's own adopted tip and the BLUE authority rejected SlotBeforeLastApplied, ending an otherwise-successful continuous run with exit 43 AFTER AddedToCurrentChain; RED chokepoint only, NO BLUE change). Prior through PHASE4-N-AE (enforced): DC-NODE-14 (recovered/forge-parent FindIntersect-only intersectability) + DC-NODE-15 (forge admissibility = durable servable tip == followed peer tip) + DC-CONS-24 (forged parent hash byte-equals the peer-visible tip) + DC-PROTO-10 (chain-sync server FindIntersect cursor -- the CE-A5 manifest closer) + 9 strengthenings (incl. CN-CONS-06 first live cross-impl acceptance, DC-WAL-02/T-REC-05 recover->follow continuity). The CE-A5 manifest: a real cardano-node 11.0.1 relay AddedToCurrentChain an Ade-forged block. Prior through PHASE4-N-AC (enforced): DC-CRYPTO-10 (the RED producer signing shell evolves the operator KES key forward to the requested KES period before signing -- kes_sign_header_advancing = kes_advance_to(period) then kes_sign_header, reusing the existing deterministic Sum6KES update; fail-closed EvolutionBackwards backwards / EvolutionExhausted beyond the key lifetime; the forge's single real KES sign is rewired to it so the forge works across KES periods, not just the minted period 0) + strengthening CN-KES-HEADER-01 (the header KES signature is over the canonical unsigned-header pre-image AND is now signed with the key evolved to the block's KES period). Prior through PHASE4-N-AB (enforced): CN-SESS-05 (outbound mux segmentation -- the GREEN session reducer segments outbound mini-protocol payloads > MAX_PAYLOAD into ordered <=MAX_PAYLOAD mux frames via the single encode_inner_frame authority and fails closed above the fixed non-configurable MAX_OUTBOUND_PAYLOAD_BYTES = 16 MiB; the outbound inverse of CN-SESS-04 inbound reassembly, so Ade can SERVE a >64 KB block) + strengthenings CN-SESS-04 + DC-SERVEMEM-01 (inbound reassembly now has a symmetric outbound segmentation; the bounded peer-driven serve range can now transmit large blocks within the bound). Prior through PHASE4-N-AA (enforced): DC-SERVEMEM-01 (bounded peer-driven serve range -- the --mode node serve caps each peer request at MAX_SERVE_RANGE_BLOCKS=256 via hash-free bounded ChainDb primitives + fail-closed; closes the PHASE4-N-U cross-slice security MEDIUM; serve-side analog of DC-LIVEMEM-01) + strengthenings DC-NODE-13 + DC-LIVEMEM-01 (durable-chain serve projection now bounded; receive-side bound now symmetric on the serve side). Prior through PHASE4-N-U (all enforced): DC-NODE-12 + DC-CONS-23 + DC-WAL-04 + T-REC-05 + DC-NODE-13 (forged-block durability -- own-forged durable admit via pump_block, forged-tip warm-start forward-replay recovery, serve-as-durable-chain projection) + strengthenings CN-CONS-07 + DC-NODE-11 (serve-provenance / monotone-gate superseded). Prior through PHASE4-N-F-G-R: DC-NODE-09 (G-K, serve-listener lifetime), CN-WIRE-10 (G-L, serve-side N2N handshake V15 cardano-node compat -- live-confirmed), CN-WIRE-11 (G-M, real-node ChainSync FindIntersect + ArrayHead), T-REC-04 + DC-CINPUT-03 (G-N, WarmStart eta0 seed-epoch sidecar), CN-WIRE-12 (G-O, feed-side BlockFetch tag-24 unwrap), DC-CINPUT-04 (G-P, feed header-validation view), DC-NODE-10 (G-Q, forge-successor evolved-spine position), DC-NODE-11 (G-R, monotone served-chain stability gate).
_ci_dirs_doc: CI scripts live as `ci/ci_check_*.sh` (ci_dirs = ["ci"]). A non-gating .github/workflows/notify-atlas.yml dispatch workflow also exists (grounding-doc -> ade-atlas rebuild trigger), but it is not an Ade invariant gate; add '.github/workflows' to ci_dirs only if/when an actual invariant gate is added there.
